summary: DeVault contrasts two approaches to dealing with reactionary bad-faith actors in FOSS communities. He critiques a detailed, measured technical response (Exhibit A) that inadvertently gives trolls ammunition for counter-arguments and concern trolling. He praises a concise, truth-speaking dismissal (Exhibit B) that denies reactionaries the engagement they seek. The post argues that FOSS community leaders should not waste time crafting long-winded technical justifications to exclude bad actors, but should instead speak plainly and ban them without ceremony. The underlying incident involves a reactionary fork of Xorg used as a vehicle for alt-right culture war politics against Linux distributions.
key_points: Detailed technical responses to bad-faith actors backfire by providing material for counter-arguments and concern trolling
The most effective response to reactionary trolls in FOSS is concise dismissal that speaks the truth directly
FOSS community leaders are not obligated to treat bad-faith agitators as equal partners deserving professional engagement
Reactionaries weaponize the X11-vs-Wayland divide to inject culture war politics into open source communities
Denying attention is a key tactic — the censored project name demonstrates the principle of not feeding the hate machine
money_quotes: Do not answer bad faith with good faith. This is a skill you need to learn in order to be an effective community leader.
If you are a community leader in FOSS, you are not obligated to waste your time coming up with a long-winded technical answer to keep nazis out of your community.
They want you to argue with them and give them attention and feed them material for their reactionary blog or whatever. Don't fall into their trap.
She probably understood at a glance that the community behind this "project" literally wants to lynch her.

The requirement for a passing grade in this class is a polite but summary dismissal, but additional credit is awarded for anyone who does not indulge far-right agitators as if they were equal partners in maintaining a sense of professional decorum.
The project itself is of little consequence; they serve our purposes today by providing us with case-studies in dealing with reactionary idiots starting shit in your community.
All they really want is attention, and you and me are going to do each other a solid by not giving them any of that directly.
key_insight: FOSS leaders should respond to bad-faith reactionary actors with blunt truth and swift bans, not detailed technical arguments that feed the trolls' attention-seeking behavior.
